results.possibleIdentity: Stanton Korey was a New York City-based filmmaker and producer active during the late 1960s, 1970s, and 1980s. He was the principal of Plus Two Productions Limited (also known as Plus Two Productions, Ltd.), a production company through which he produced and directed a wide range of media including television commercials, documentaries, visual aids, and industrial films. Korey is identified as a filmmaker with technical expertise in film production and may have been associated with the non-profit organization Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.), which facilitated collaborations between artists, engineers, and scientists. His professional circle included literary and media figures such as author and CBS executive Daniel Stern. In his later career, Korey's credits included serving as a producer for films such as "Young Lady Chatterley II" (1985). He resided in Manhattan, where he was known to drive a maroon Mercedes and owned a Golden Retriever named Barney. Korey passed away in the late 20th century, with an obituary published in the New York Times citing a blood infection as the cause of death.
